tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting possible renal dysfunction and/or hepatic involvement. Serum glucose and HbA1c are within or below normal, making diabetes unlikely. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal and liver function, and correlation with clinical findings for underlying causes such as glomerular disease or hepatic pathology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
